name: A redundant second bond is harmless
statement: Adding a second bond at the subpanel is redundant — nothing actually changes during normal operation.
correction: Redundant paths in a circuit aren't passive — they're parallel. Two conductors tied at both ends share current proportional to their conductance. The moment the second bond is in, every bonded metal raceway and EGC starts carrying continuous load current — current the EGC was meant to carry only briefly during a fault. They were never sized or insulated for that, and the voltage drops they produce show up as touch voltage on appliance cases and water pipes.
